Home /echomq/cache/single-flight-and-jittered-ttl
EchoMQ / The Cache / Single-flight & jittered TTL

The Cache · module 02

One fill
per herd

A cache that does not stampede and does not grow without bound.

Module 01 built the declared near-cache and showed how a read resolves across the two layers. Module 02 is the second half: the discipline that keeps it cheap under a herd and bounded under pressure. Concurrent misses on the same key coalesce onto a single in-flight load — one fill, not a thousand. Expiry is jittered so a cohort never dies in step, and a sweeper reclaims dead rows on a fixed tick. And when the table is full and no expired rows exist, a fill still serves its caller from L2 and the loader: the cache degrades to pass-through, it never fails. All three laws are in EchoStore.Table, real shipped code.

One fill per herd The dives References

A herd, one flight

Pick how many callers miss simultaneously on the same key and watch the owner's mailbox coalesce them into a single loader call. The :coalesced counter records each waiter that did not start its own flight. Pick a path to see the call sequence and the counter update.

The owner's mailbox is the serialization point — the first {:fill, id} starts a flight; every other appends. The loader runs once; every waiter reads that one answer. The :coalesced counter records every saved loader call.

The dives

Three dives, each a law from EchoStore.Table: the coalescing flight, the jittered clock, and the graceful degrade under pressure.

01built

One fill per herd

handle_call({:fill, id}) coalesces concurrent misses onto one flight; the spawn_monitor'd task GETs, loads, and replies to every waiter.
02built

Jittered expiry

expires_at/1 draws each row's death from ttl ± spread; the sweeper reclaims dead rows on a fixed sweep_ms tick.
03built

The full cache degrades

insert/4 tries to reclaim expired rows before skipping; a skip is a pass-through, not a failure. stats/1 makes the rate observable.

References

Sources

  • Erlang/OTP — the ets module — the read-concurrent L1 table the flight fills.
  • Valkey — Cluster specification — the {table} hashtag and one-slot discipline.
  • Valkey — GET — the L2 probe a flight issues on a miss.
  • Valkey — SET — the framed version <> value write after a fill.
  • Helland — Life Beyond Distributed Transactions — the entity addressed by a key, cached close to its use.
  • King — Announcing Snowflake — the mint-time id every cached value is framed with.

Related in this course

  • The Cache — the chapter landing; module 01 is the entry point.
  • Cache-aside, two layers — module 01: the declared tiers and the read path.
  • The Protocol — the keyspace the ecc: prefix stands beside.
  • The Queue — the other pillar over the same wire.
  • The Branded Component System · the Store — the manuscript chapter this pillar realizes.
  • Redis Patterns Applied · R1 Caching — the patterns this module applies in depth.
